attack_chain.description: Attackers authenticated against Microsoft Entra ID using captured session tokens, bypassing MFA to access the M365 tenant.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: authenticated against Entra ID using captured session tokens. This technique not only bypassed multi-factor authentication (MFA)

evidence_chunks.quote: authenticated against Entra ID using captured session tokens. This technique not only bypassed multi-factor authentication (MFA)

attack_chain.description: Attackers used harvested AWS access keys for direct API access and used compromised Entra ID users to reach the AWS web console.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: AWS access keys were discovered on the compromised devices as well, giving the attackers two ways into the AWS environment—through direct API access and the web console via compromised Entra ID users

evidence_chunks.quote: AWS access keys were discovered on the compromised devices as well, giving the attackers two ways into the AWS environment—through direct API access and the web console via compromised Entra ID users
AWS access keys were discovered on the compromised devices as well, giving the attackers two ways into the AWS environment—through direct API access and the web console via compromised Entra ID users

attack_chain.description: Attackers modified a Lambda function that had the ability to execute code on previously inaccessible EC2 instances, achieving privilege escalation and lateral movement.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: modifying a Lambda function that had the ability to execute code on several previously inaccessible EC2 instances

modifying a Lambda function that had the ability to execute code on several previously inaccessible EC2 instances

attack_chain.description: Using control over the EC2 instances, attackers crafted fraudulent API calls that provided access to the targeted critical assets.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: craft fraudulent API calls that finally gave them access to the critical assets they were seeking

evidence_chunks.quote: craft fraudulent API calls that finally gave them access to the critical assets they were seeking
craft fraudulent API calls that finally gave them access to the critical assets they were seeking

initial_access_vector: Social engineering on LinkedIn/WhatsApp by a state-sponsored actor that convinced developers to run infostealer code, harvesting AWS access keys and Entra ID session tokens from corporate laptops.
persistence_mechanism: Use of stolen long-term AWS access keys and captured Entra ID session tokens for continued access.
privilege_escalation: Modification of a Lambda function whose execution role could run code on previously inaccessible EC2 instances, letting the attacker pivot from limited AWS access to control of sensitive EC2 workloads.
